questions.text: Which of the following are not polymer?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Proteins are formed by long chains of amino acids, which are their monomers. Hence, they are polymers.
Polysaccharides consist of repeated units of monosaccharides, making them polymers.
Lipids do not have a repetitive monomer structure and are not considered polymers. Their structures are diverse and include components like fatty acids and glycerol.
Nucleic acids are formed from long chains of nucleotides, their monomers, thus classifying them as polymers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Lipids. Unlike proteins, polysaccharides, and nucleic acids, lipids do not consist of repeating monomer units and therefore are not classified as polymers. Proteins are polymers because they are made up of amino acids linked in chains. Polysaccharides are polymers of monosaccharides, and nucleic acids are polymers of nucleotides, each consisting of a series of repeated structural units.

questions.text: A typical fat molecule is made up of :
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: One glycerol and one fatty acid molecule
options.explanation: A typical fat molecule is made up of three fatty acids and one glycerol molecule.

options.text: Three glycerol and three fatty acid molecules
options.explanation: A typical fat molecule is made up of three fatty acids and one glycerol molecule.

options.text: Three glycerol molecules and one fatty acid molecule
options.explanation: A typical fat molecule is made up of three fatty acids and one glycerol molecule.

options.text: One glycerol and three fatty acid molecules.
options.explanation: Neutral or true fats are triglycerides that are formed by esterification of three molecules of fatty acids with one molecule of trihydric alcohol, glycerol (glycerine or trihydroxy propane).

questions.explanation: Neutral or true fats are triglycerides that are formed by esterification of three molecules of fatty acids with one molecule of trihydric alcohol, glycerol (glycerine or trihydroxy propane).

questions.text: Most of the monosaccharides form a _ when in solution.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Although, monosaccharides are present I'm straight chain form but in solution, they form ring structures. So, this option is not correct.

No, individual monosaccharide molecules do not form branched structures in solution. A single monosaccharide molecule has an unbranched (linear) carbon backbone, which in an aqueous solution exists in equilibrium with a ring-shaped (cyclic) structure. So, this option is not correct.

Yes, monosaccharides, particularly those with five or more carbon atoms (pentoses and hexoses), readily form ring structures in an aqueous solution. This is the predominant and most stable form, while the linear (open-chain) form exists in only a very small percentage at any given time (e.g., less than 0.02% for glucose). So, this option is correct.

Monosaccharides do not form complex "folded" structures in the same way that large macromolecules like proteins do.Instead, individual monosaccharide molecules exist in a simple, dynamic equilibrium between a linear (open-chain) form and one or more stable cyclic (ring) forms in an aqueous solution. The ring structures are generally more energetically favorable and thus predominate in biological systems. So, this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: Monosaccharides, particularly those with five or more carbon atoms (pentoses and hexoses), readily form ring structures in an aqueous solution. This is the predominant and most stable form, while the linear (open-chain) form exists in only a very small percentage at any given time (e.g., less than 0.02% for glucose).

questions.text: Ribose forms a _ cornered ring.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as ribose forms a five cornered ring.
This option is incorrect as ribose forms a five cornered ring.
This option is incorrect as ribose forms a five cornered ring.

Yes, ribose forms a five-membered ring, which is also called a furanose ring. This ring consists of four carbon atoms and one oxygen atom.Structure: In aqueous solution, ribose primarily exists in its cyclic furanose form. The ring is made up of four carbon atoms from the original five-carbon chain and one oxygen atom.Numbering: The carbon atoms in the ring are numbered 1 through 4, with the fifth carbon (C5) attached to the fourth carbon and extending outside the ring. Function: This five-membered ring structure is crucial in biological molecules like RNA, where ribose forms the backbone with a phosphate group. So, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: Ribose forms a five-membered ring, which is also called a furanose ring. This ring consists of four carbon atoms and one oxygen atom. Structure: In aqueous solution, ribose primarily exists in its cyclic furanose form. The ring is made up of four carbon atoms from the original five-carbon chain and one oxygen atom.Numbering: The carbon atoms in the ring are numbered 1 through 4, with the fifth carbon (C5) attached to the fourth carbon and extending outside the ring.Function: This five-membered ring structure is crucial in biological molecules like RNA, where ribose forms the backbone with a phosphate group.

questions.text: Glucose forms a six cornered ring called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glucopyranose is the correct answer as it refers to the six-membered ring structure of glucose. This form is derived from the structure of pyran, a six-membered ring compound, and is the most stable form of glucose in solution.
Fructofuranose is incorrect because it is a five-membered ring structure of fructose, not glucose. It consists of four carbon atoms and one oxygen atom.
Ribofuranose is incorrect as it represents a five-membered ring structure of ribose, a different sugar. Ribose contains five carbon atoms.
Deoxyribofuranose is incorrect because it is a form of deoxyribose, a component of DNA, and forms a five-membered ring. It is neither glucose nor does it form a six-membered ring.

questions.explanation: Glucose forms a six-membered ring known as glucopyranose when it cyclizes. This structure is named after pyran, a six-membered ring compound, making it the most prevalent and stable form of glucose in aqueous solution. The other options—fructofuranose, ribofuranose, and deoxyribofuranose—are all furanose forms, implying they have five-membered rings. These structures correspond to different sugars or modified sugars, not glucose.

questions.text: These are comparatively less sweet in taste and less soluble in water:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monosaccharides are simple sugars that are highly soluble in water and generally sweet in taste. They consist of single sugar molecules and are fundamental units of carbohydrates.

Oligosaccharides consist of 3 to 10 monosaccharide units and are known for being less sweet and less soluble in water compared to monosaccharides and disaccharides. They have more complex structures, which reduces their sweetness and solubility.
Disaccharides are composed of two monosaccharide molecules. They are generally sweet and soluble in water, similar to monosaccharides, making them unsuitable as the correct answer.

Polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates composed of many monosaccharide units. They are not sweet and are less soluble in water than other carbohydrate types, but they are more complex than oligosaccharides.

questions.explanation: Oligosaccharides are the correct answer because they consist of multiple monosaccharide units, typically ranging from 3 to 10. This complexity leads to a reduction in the sweetness and solubility in water compared to simpler carbohydrates like monosaccharides and disaccharides. Monosaccharides and disaccharides are generally sweet and highly soluble in water, whereas polysaccharides, although also less sweet and soluble, are much more complex than oligosaccharides.

questions.text: _ gives blue color with iodine.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Glycogen is a highly branched storage polysaccharide found in animals. It interacts with iodine to produce a reddish-brown color, not blue.
Cellulose is a linear polysaccharide that forms the structural component of plant cell walls. It does not change color significantly with iodine, remaining colorless or slightly yellow.
Dextrin consists of partially hydrolyzed starch molecules and may produce a yellowish or reddish-brown color with iodine, but not blue.
Starches are composed of amylose and amylopectin. Amylose forms a helical structure that traps iodine molecules, leading to a blue color, which is characteristic of starch in iodine tests.

questions.explanation: The iodine test is used to identify the presence of starch, which turns blue-black when iodine is added. This color change occurs because iodine fits into the helical structure of amylose, a component of starch, forming a charge-transfer complex. Glycogen and dextrin do not produce a blue color with iodine; glycogen gives a reddish-brown color, and dextrin may appear yellowish or reddish-brown. Cellulose does not yield a significant color change with iodine.

questions.text: Lipids store double amount of energy, as compared to the equal amount of carbohydrates, because of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lipids contain long hydrocarbon chains with an abundance of C-H bonds. These bonds store a significant amount of energy, which is released when they are metabolized. This high proportion of energy-rich C-H bonds is why lipids store more energy compared to carbohydrates.

While some lipids may contain nitrogen, especially in types like phospholipids, it is not the C-N bonds that contribute to the high energy storage capacity of lipids. The energy storage is primarily due to C-H bonds.
Carbohydrates have a higher proportion of C-O bonds, not lipids. The oxygen in carbohydrates makes them more oxidized, whereas lipids are more reduced, allowing more energy release during metabolism.
C-O-P bonds are typical in certain molecules like phospholipids, but they are not predominant in the energy storage role of lipids. Lipids' energy density is not related to these bonds.

questions.explanation: Lipids store double the amount of energy compared to an equal amount of carbohydrates because they are rich in C-H bonds. These bonds are high in energy and are prevalent in the long hydrocarbon chains typical of lipids. When these bonds are broken during metabolism, they release a significant amount of energy. Other types of bonds mentioned, such as C-N, C-O, and C-O-P, do not play a primary role in the energy storage capacity of lipids. Carbohydrates, with more C-O bonds, are already partially oxidized, and thus store less energy per gram.

questions.text: A phosphatidic acid upon combining with a choline gives rise to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because phosphatidic acid is a precursor that participates in the reaction rather than being the product.
This option is correct. Phosphatidic acid reacts with choline to form phosphatidylcholine, a vital component of cell membranes involved in various metabolic processes.
This option is too general. While phosphatidylcholine is a type of phospholipid, the reaction specifically between phosphatidic acid and choline yields phosphatidylcholine.
This option is incorrect because phosphatidylserine forms when phosphatidic acid reacts with serine, not choline.

questions.explanation: When phosphatidic acid combines with choline, it specifically forms phosphatidylcholine. This compound is essential for the structure and function of cell membranes and plays a role in lipid metabolism and cell signaling. The other options are incorrect:Phosphatidic acid: It is a reactant, not a product.Phospholipids: This category is too broad; the reaction with choline specifically results in phosphatidylcholine.Phosphatidylserine: This product results from the reaction with serine, not choline.

questions.text: Biological macromolecules are polymers that are formed when _ are joined by a _ reaction.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Monomers are the basic building blocks of biological macromolecules. They combine through a dehydration (or condensation) reaction, which involves the removal of a water molecule to form a polymer. This is the correct process for forming biological macromolecules.
Reduction reactions involve the gain of electrons or the decrease in oxidation state, which is not involved in the formation of biological macromolecules. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

Multimers are composed of multiple units, which are themselves formed from monomers. It is monomers, not multimers, that are joined through a dehydration reaction to form biological macromolecules. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
Hydrolysis is the process that breaks down polymers into monomers by using water, which is opposite to the dehydration process that forms macromolecules. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Biological macromolecules are large molecules essential for life, formed from smaller units called monomers. The monomers are linked together through a dehydration reaction, also known as a condensation reaction, where a water molecule is removed during the bonding process. Option A is correct because it accurately describes this process. Option B is incorrect because reduction reactions are not involved in forming macromolecules. Option C is incorrect because it incorrectly uses 'multimers' instead of 'monomers.' Option D is incorrect because hydrolysis is the process of breaking down polymers, not forming them.

questions.text: The inhibitor which closely resembles the substrate in its molecular structure and inhibits the enzyme activity by binding to the active site of the enzyme is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Feedback inhibition occurs when the end product of a metabolic pathway binds to an enzyme (often at an allosteric site) to regulate the pathway. It doesn’t require structural resemblance to the substrate.
A non-competitive inhibitor binds to a site other than the active site, changing the enzyme’s shape and reducing activity. It does not mimic the substrate’s structure.
An allosteric modulator binds at the allosteric site, not at the active site. It alters the enzyme’s function either positively or negatively, but it doesn’t resemble the substrate.

In competitive inhibition, the inhibitor closely resembles the substrate and competes for the enzyme’s active site. While bound, it prevents the real substrate from binding, thereby reducing enzyme activity. This inhibition can be overcome by increasing substrate concentration.

questions.explanation: In competitive inhibition, the inhibitor (I) closely resembles the real substrate (S), and is regarded as a substrate analogue. The inhibitor competes with substrate and binds at the active site of the enzyme but does not undergo any catalysis. As long as the competitive inhibitor holds the active site, the enzyme is not available for the substrate to bind. A competitive inhibitor diminishes the rate of catalysis by reducing the proportion of enzyme molecules bound to a substrate. At any given inhibitor concentration, competitive inhibition can be relieved by increasing the substrate concentration.So, the correct answer is 'Competitive inhibitor'.

questions.text: The following statements are about enzymes: 1. They are globular proteins except ribo-enzyme.2. They can be inhibited3. They are formed in the smooth endoplasmic reticulum 4. There are only found attached to Plasma membranes in the cell Which statements are correct for all enzymes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The statement “all enzymes are proteins” is not correct.While most enzymes are indeed protein in nature, exceptions exist.RNA molecules with catalytic activity, called ribozymes, also function as enzymes.Example: the ribosome’s peptidyl-transferase activity is carried out by rRNA.Some synthetic DNA molecules (deoxyribozymes) also show catalytic roles.Hence, not every enzyme can be classified as a protein.Therefore, any option containing this statement, like A (1 and 4), is incorrect.

Competitive inhibitors are molecules that closely resemble the substrate of an enzyme. They compete with the substrate for binding to the active site of the enzyme. When a competitive inhibitor binds to the active site, it prevents the substrate from binding and, as a result, inhibits the enzyme's normal catalytic activity. Enzymes are not exclusively found attached to plasma membranes in cells. Enzymes are proteins that act as catalysts to facilitate and accelerate biochemical reactions, and they can be found in various cellular compartments, not just associated with the plasma membrane. The cellular location of enzymes depends on their specific functions and the processes they are involved in.

Enzymes are a type of protein, and they have a three-dimensional, globular shape. This shape is crucial for their function, as it allows them to interact specifically with their substrates. Competitive inhibitors are molecules that closely resemble the substrate of an enzyme. They compete with the substrate for binding to the active site of the enzyme. When a competitive inhibitor binds to the active site, it prevents the substrate from binding and, as a result, inhibits the enzyme's normal catalytic activity.

This statement is false. Enzymes are not exclusively found attached to plasma membranes in cells. Enzymes are proteins that act as catalysts to facilitate and accelerate biochemical reactions, and they can be found in various cellular compartments, not just associated with the plasma membrane. The cellular location of enzymes depends on their specific functions and the processes they are involved in.

questions.explanation: Enzymes are a type of protein, and they have a three-dimensional, globular shape. This shape is crucial for their function, as it allows them to interact specifically with their substrates. Competitive inhibitors are molecules that closely resemble the substrate of an enzyme. They compete with the substrate for binding to the active site of the enzyme. When a competitive inhibitor binds to the active site, it prevents the substrate from binding and, as a result, inhibits the enzyme's normal catalytic activity.

questions.text: An activated enzyme made of polypeptide chain and a cofactor is?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A substrate is a molecule that an enzyme acts upon during a chemical reaction. Substrates bind to an enzyme's active site and are transformed into a product. Therefore, it is not the correct answer.
A coenzyme is a type of cofactor that is an organic molecule, such as a vitamin or a nucleotide, that binds to an enzyme to help it carry out its function.
An apoenzyme is an inactive enzyme without its cofactor. Apoenzymes require the binding of a cofactor to become active.

An activated enzyme made of a polypeptide chain and a cofactor is referred to as a "holoenzyme." The holoenzyme is the complete, catalytically active form of an enzyme. The holoenzyme structure ensures that the enzyme is in its active conformation and is capable of facilitating the desired biochemical reactions.

questions.explanation: An activated enzyme made of a polypeptide chain and a cofactor is referred to as a "holoenzyme." The holoenzyme is the complete, catalytically active form of an enzyme. The holoenzyme structure ensures that the enzyme is in its active conformation and is capable of facilitating the desired biochemical reactions.

questions.text: Which of the following factors affect enzyme activity?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Temperature affects enzyme activity as higher temperatures generally increase reaction rates, but extreme heat may denature enzymes, reducing their activity.
pH levels can alter an enzyme's shape and functionality. Each enzyme has an optimal pH range where it performs best.
Enzyme activity can increase with substrate concentration, but only until the enzymes become saturated and the reaction rate plateaus.
Radiation can damage enzymes through oxidative stress, affecting their ability to function properly, especially at higher doses.
Each listed factor influences enzyme activity in different ways, making 'All of these' the correct answer.

questions.explanation: All of these factors can influence enzyme activity:Temperature: Enzymes generally work faster with rising temperatures until they reach an optimum point. Beyond this, high temperatures can cause denaturation.pH: Each enzyme has an optimal pH range. Deviations from this range can alter the enzyme's shape and reduce its efficiency.Concentration of substrate: Increasing substrate concentration can boost reaction rates up to a point of saturation.Radiation: While not typically considered an immediate factor, radiation can cause oxidative damage to enzymes, impeding their function.Thus, all these factors are relevant, making Option E the correct choice. Options A, B, C, and D describe individual factors, but only 'All of these' encompasses the full scope of influences on enzyme activity.

questions.text: An enzyme increases the speed of a reaction:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Enzymes do not add activation energy requirements. Instead, they lower the activation energy needed, enabling the reaction to proceed faster.

options.text: By lowering the activation energy requirements.
options.explanation: Enzymes work by lowering the activation energy of reactions, making it easier for the reaction to occur and thereby increasing the reaction speed.

options.text: By altering the equilibrium of the reaction.
options.explanation: Enzymes do not change the equilibrium position of a reaction; they only help the reaction reach equilibrium faster by lowering the activation energy.

options.text: By changing the concentration of products.
options.explanation: Enzymes do not directly change the concentration of products. They speed up the rate at which equilibrium is reached, but do not affect the final concentrations at equilibrium.

questions.explanation: Enzymes serve as biological catalysts that increase the rate of chemical reactions by lowering the activation energy required for the reaction to proceed. Activation energy is the minimum amount of energy needed to initiate a reaction. By providing an alternative reaction pathway with a lower activation energy, enzymes allow reactions to occur more rapidly.Option A is incorrect because enzymes do not add to the activation energy requirements; they reduce them. Option C is incorrect because enzymes do not alter the equilibrium position of the reaction; they only accelerate the rate at which equilibrium is reached. Option D is incorrect because while enzymes affect the rate of reaction, they do not directly change the concentration of products.

questions.text: The optimal pH for the working of enzymes is?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A pH of 5 is acidic, and while a few specialized enzymes (like some found in fungi or in the stomach) can work at this level, most enzymes in the human body would not function properly at such a low pH. At pH 5, many cellular enzymes would begin to lose their proper shape (denature) and their activity would be reduced. Therefore, this is not the general optimal pH for enzymes.

This is the correct answer. Most enzymes in the human body work best at or near neutral pH, and pH 7.5 is very close to this neutral range. Enzymes inside cells are adapted to the slightly neutral environment of the cytoplasm and body fluids, where they maintain their structure and catalytic efficiency. That is why for the majority of enzymes, pH around 7–7.5 is considered optimal.

A pH of 9 is alkaline, and while some enzymes (like those in the small intestine such as pancreatic enzymes) may tolerate or function in slightly basic conditions, a strong alkaline environment denatures most cellular enzymes. Since most body enzymes are not adapted to such a high pH, this is not the general optimum for enzyme activity.

A pH of 3 is strongly acidic, and this is only optimal for a few specialized enzymes such as pepsin in the stomach, which is adapted to the highly acidic environment caused by hydrochloric acid. However, for the majority of enzymes in the human body, such an acidic condition would denature them. Hence, this cannot be considered the general optimal pH for enzymes overall.

questions.explanation: pH 7.5 is closest to neutral and most enzymes would denature in other values of pH.

questions.text: Enzyme-substrate specificity is a result of which property of enzymes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The primary structure deals with the sequential arrangements of amino acids in a protein chain. The primary structure determines the three-dimensional shape of the proteins. Hence option A is incorrect.

The secondary structures of proteins help us to predict the tertiary structure of proteins because determining structure based on arrangements of amino acids is insufficient. The protein structure and folding are estimated by secondary structure. It cannot determine the specificity of the substrate hence option B is incorrect.

The specificity of the substrate of the protein structure is determined by the tertiary structure. The proteins have a very specific shape due to their tertiary structure which plays an important role in the lock and key mechanism of the enzymes. Hence option C is correct.
The quaternary structure of proteins deals with the association of several protein chains. This is closely related to the functions of proteins. Hence option D is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The specificity of the substrate of the protein structure is determined by the tertiary structure. The proteins have a very specific shape due to their tertiary structure which plays important role in the lock and key mechanism of the enzymes. Hence option c is correct.The primary structure deals with the sequential arrangements of amino acids in a protein chain. The primary structure determines the three-dimensional shape of the proteins. Hence option A is incorrect.The quaternary structure of proteins deals with the association of several protein chains. This is closely related to the functions of proteins. Hence option D is incorrect.The secondary structures of protein help us to predict the tertiary structure of proteins because determining structure based on arrangements of amino acids is insufficient. The protein structure and folding are estimated by secondary structure. It cannot determine the specificity of the substrate hence option B is incorrect.

questions.text: A cofactor made of an inorganic ion which is detachable is called a _.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A is wrong because prostrate group are those when cofactor is covalently bonded with protein part.These are permanently bonded and does not detach after the completion of reaction.
B is wrong because coenzyme are those when cofactor is loosely bonded with protein part.These are organic in nature . Coenzyme are derivatives of vitamins.
C is correct option because activator are inorganic cofactors which can be easily deattached from enzymes.These are only attached to enzymes when substrate gets bind.
C is correct option because activator are inorganic cofactors which can be easily deattached from enzymes.These are only attached to enzymes when substrate gets bind.

questions.explanation: C is correct option because activator are inorganic cofactors which can be easily deattached from enzymes.These are only attached to enzymes when substrate gets bind.

questions.text: All enzymes are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Enzymes are mainly globular proteins - protein molecules where the tertiary structure has given the molecule a generally rounded, ball shape

A fibrous protein forms long protein filaments, which are shaped like rods or wires. Fibrous proteins are structural or storage proteins that are typically inert and water-insoluble. A fibrous protein occurs as an aggregate due to hydrophobic side chains that protrude from the molecule.

Glycoproteins are molecules that comprise protein and carbohydrate chains that are involved in many physiological functions including immunity. Many viruses have glycoproteins that help them enter bodily cells, but can also serve to be important therapeutic or preventative targets.

Lipoproteins are particles made of protein and fats (lipids). They carry cholesterol through your bloodstream to your cells. The two main groups of lipoproteins are called HDL (high-density lipoprotein) or "good" cholesterol and LDL (low-density lipoprotein) or "bad" cholesterol. Lipoprotein (a) is a type of LDL.

questions.explanation: Enzymes are mainly globular proteins - protein molecules where the tertiary structure has given the molecule a generally rounded, ball shape

questions.text: Which cell organelle is present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cell wall surrounds the plasma membrane of plant cells and provides tensile strength and protection against mechanical and osmotic stress. It also allows cells to develop turgor pressure, which is the pressure of the cell contents against the cell wall.

Mitochondria are membrane-bound cell organelles (mitochondrion, singular) that generate most of the chemical energy needed to power the cell's biochemical reactions. Chemical energy produced by the mitochondria is stored in a small molecule called adenosine triphosphate (ATP).

A ribosome is the cellular machinery responsible for making proteins. There are many ribosomes in each cell, each made up of two subunits. These two subunits lock around the messenger RNA and then travel along the length of the messenger RNA molecule reading each three-letter codon.

The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a large, dynamic structure that serves many roles in the cell including calcium storage, protein synthesis and lipid metabolism. The diverse functions of the ER are performed by distinct domains; consisting of tubules, sheets and the nuclear envelope.

questions.explanation: Prokaryotes and eukaryotes are the two different types of cells. Eukaryotic cells contain membrane-bound organelles, such as the nucleus, endoplasmic reticulum, mitochondria while prokaryotic cells do not but the ribosome is the only organelle that can be seen in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. Prokaryotic and eukaryotic ribosomes perform the same functions that is protein synthesis, however, eukaryotic ribosomes are much larger than prokaryotic ones.

questions.text: Ribosomal RNA is actively synthesized in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Factually incorrect, because lysosomes are known as suicidal sacs, not a RNA factory.
This option is correct because the ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is synthesized and stored in the nucleolus.
It is untrue because the ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is synthesized and stored in the nucleolus, and not in the nucleoplasm.
This option is incorrect because Ribosomes are the protein factories, not RNA factories.

questions.explanation: The nucleolus is the center for the synthesis of ribosomal RNA (rRNA) that forms ribosomal subunits. Ribosomal proteins migrate to the nucleolus from their assembly sites in the cytoplasm and are packaged into ribonucleoproteins. These return to the cytoplasm where they become mature ribosome particles.

questions.text: A protoplast is a cell
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cell division happens when a parent cell divides into two or more cells called daughter cells. Cell division usually occurs as part of a larger cell cycle. All cells reproduce by splitting into two, where each parental cell gives rise to two daughter cells.

Protoplasts are isolated cells produced by removing the surrounding cell wall either by mechanical means or by the use of cell wall degrading enzymes. Removal of the cell wall leaves the protoplast surrounded by the plasmalemma membrane.

A eukaryotic cell is separated from the extracellular environment by a plasma membrane composed of a phospholipid bilayer containing proteins that regulate transit of molecules into and out of the cell. Loss of this barrier function can lead to compromised cellular homeostasis and death of the cell.

Prokaryotes are organisms whose cells lack a nucleus and other organelles. Prokaryotes are divided into two distinct groups: the bacteria and the archaea, which scientists believe have unique evolutionary lineages.

questions.explanation: Protoplasts are cells with the plasma membrane, cytoplasm and nucleus, which have had their cell wall removed by the action of enzymes. Cellulase enzymes digest the cellulose in plant cell walls while pectinase enzymes break down the pectin holding cells together.

questions.text: The rigidity of leaves and younger parts of the plants is contributed by?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is correct because vacuoles maintain turgor pressure, which is essential for the rigidity and structural support of plant cells, especially in leaves and younger parts of the plant.
Mitochondria are known as the powerhouses of the cell, responsible for energy production, but they do not provide structural support or contribute to plant rigidity.
Actin is part of the cytoskeleton and plays a role in cell movement and structure, but it does not contribute to the rigidity of plant cells in the way vacuoles do through turgor pressure.
Glyoxysomes are involved in the conversion of fats into sugars, particularly in seedlings, and do not contribute to the rigidity of leaves or younger parts of plants.

questions.explanation: Vacuoles are crucial for maintaining turgor pressure within plant cells, which is a key factor in providing rigidity and support to leaves and younger parts of plants. This turgor pressure is created by the vacuole's ability to store water and other compounds, leading to an outward pressure on the cell wall. In contrast, mitochondria are involved in energy production, actin is part of the cytoskeleton, and glyoxysomes are involved in metabolic processes, but none of these structures contribute directly to the rigidity of plant tissues.

questions.text: Cell wall shows:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cell wall is completely permeable, allowing most substances to pass freely in and out of the cell. Its primary function is structural support, not regulation of substance passage.
This option is incorrect. Semipermeability refers to selective passage of substances, a property of the cell membrane, not the cell wall.
This option is incorrect. Differential permeability suggests selective transport, which is characteristic of the cell membrane, not the cell wall.
This option is incorrect. An impermeable barrier would not allow passage of any substances, which is not true for the cell wall.

questions.explanation: The cell wall is completely permeable, meaning it does not restrict the movement of substances into or out of the cell. This property is crucial for maintaining the structural integrity and support of the cell while allowing necessary exchanges with the environment. In contrast, the cell membrane is semipermeable or differentially permeable, meaning it selectively allows substances to pass through. Therefore, options suggesting semipermeability, differential permeability, or impermeability are incorrect.

questions.text: Ribosomes were discovered by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Camillo Golgi is known for discovering the Golgi apparatus, an organelle involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins. He did not discover ribosomes.
Keith Porter is credited with pioneering the use of electron microscopy in cell biology, but he did not discover ribosomes.
Edmund De Robertis is known for his work in developmental biology, particularly in relation to vertebrate development, but he was not involved in the discovery of ribosomes.
George Palade discovered ribosomes in animal cells and his significant contributions to cell biology earned him a Nobel Prize. Ribosomes are also known as Palade particles.

questions.explanation: George Palade discovered ribosomes in animal cells in 1955, leading to them being referred to as Palade particles. The other options, Golgi, Porter, and De Robertis, although influential in their respective fields, were not responsible for the discovery of ribosomes. Golgi is known for the Golgi apparatus, Porter for electron microscopy, and De Robertis for developmental biology.

questions.text: Fluid mosaic model of cell membrane was put forward by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The Danielli-Davson model is an earlier cell membrane model that suggested a protein-lipid sandwich, which is less accurate compared to the fluid mosaic model.
Singer and Nicolson proposed the fluid mosaic model in 1972, which is currently the most accepted model for cell membrane structure, describing proteins embedded in a fluid lipid bilayer.
Garner and Allard are not associated with any major cell membrane model. This option is incorrect in the context of the fluid mosaic model.
Watson and Crick are famous for discovering the structure of DNA, not for any cell membrane model.

questions.explanation: The fluid-mosaic model of the cell membrane was proposed by Singer and Nicolson in 1972. This model describes the cell membrane as a dynamic and fluid structure with proteins embedded in or associated with a lipid bilayer. The proteins can move laterally within the lipid layer, contributing to the 'mosaic' nature. This model accounts for the semi-permeability and functionality of the membrane. Other options, such as Danielli and Davson, presented earlier models, which have been largely replaced by the fluid-mosaic model. Garner and Allard are not relevant to this context, and Watson and Crick are known for their work on DNA structure, not cell membranes.

questions.text: The latest model for plasma membrane is called the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The lamellar model is an outdated model that was proposed in the 1930s. It suggested that the plasma membrane consists of multiple layers of lipid molecules arranged in a regular, repeating pattern. However, this model has been replaced by more accurate and dynamic models.

The unit membrane model is also an outdated model that proposes that all cellular membranes have a similar basic structure. It suggested that all membranes are made up of a single layer of lipid molecules sandwiched between two layers of protein molecules. However, this model is oversimplified and not representative of the actual complexity of cellular membranes.

The fluid mosaic model is the most widely accepted and current model for the plasma membrane. In 1972, S.J. Singer and G.L. Nicolson proposed this model. According to this model, the plasma membrane consists of a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. The lipid bilayer is fluid, meaning the individual lipid molecules can move laterally within the membrane. The proteins are not static but form a dynamic mosaic pattern floating within the lipid bilayer. The fluidity and mosaic arrangement of lipids and proteins in the membrane allow various functions, such as cell signaling, transport, and maintaining cell structure.

The term "molecular lipid model" is not commonly used to describe the structure of the plasma membrane. It may refer to various aspects of lipid molecules and their interactions in the membrane, but it is not a widely recognized model for the entire plasma membrane structure.

questions.explanation: The fluid mosaic model is the most widely accepted and current model for the plasma membrane. In 1972, S.J. Singer and G.L. Nicolson proposed this model. According to this model, the plasma membrane consists of a phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins. The lipid bilayer is fluid, meaning the individual lipid molecules can move laterally within the membrane. The proteins are not static but form a dynamic mosaic pattern floating within the lipid bilayer. The fluidity and mosaic arrangement of lipids and proteins in the membrane allow various functions, such as cell signaling, transport, and maintaining cell structure.

questions.text: Nucleoproteins are synthesised in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The nucleoplasm is the semi-fluid substance within the nucleus where various vital processes occur, such as DNA replication and RNA transcription. However, the initial synthesis of proteins, including nucleoproteins, predominantly takes place in the cytoplasm before these proteins are transported to the nucleus.

The nuclear envelope serves as a barrier that encases the nucleus and regulates the exchange of substances between the nucleus and the cytoplasm. It does not serve as the site for the synthesis of proteins, including nucleoproteins.

The nucleolus is known for ribosome production, specifically the assembly of rRNA and ribosomal proteins into ribosomal subunits. Although it plays a significant role in the production of rRNA components of ribosomes, it is not the primary site for the synthesis of nucleoproteins as a whole.

This is the correct option. The cytoplasm is where the majority of protein synthesis occurs, including the synthesis of nucleoproteins. Once synthesized, these proteins may be transported to the nucleus or other cellular locations where they perform important functions related to genetic material.

questions.explanation: The cytoplasm is the primary site for protein synthesis in the cell, including the synthesis of nucleoproteins, which are complexes of proteins and nucleic acids. These proteins are synthesized in the cytoplasm and then transported to their functional locations within the cell, such as the nucleus. The nucleoplasm is involved in processes like DNA replication and RNA synthesis but not in the initial synthesis of proteins. The nuclear envelope acts as a boundary and mediator for transport, while the nucleolus specializes in ribosome assembly, not the synthesis of nucleoproteins.

questions.text: Polyribosomes are aggregates of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While ribosomes are composed of rRNA and proteins, polyribosomes specifically refer to multiple ribosomes attached to a single mRNA strand, not just ribosomes and rRNA.
This option is incorrect. Polyribosomes are not made up solely of rRNA. They consist of several ribosomes translating a single mRNA strand, which involves both mRNA and ribosomal components.
This option is incorrect. Peroxisomes are distinct organelles involved in metabolic processes, not related to the aggregation of ribosomes or protein synthesis directly.

options.text: Several ribosomes held together by a string of mRNA
options.explanation: This option is correct. Polyribosomes, or polysomes, are complexes where multiple ribosomes are attached to a single mRNA molecule, facilitating efficient protein synthesis by simultaneously translating the same mRNA strand.

questions.explanation: Polyribosomes, also known as polysomes, are clusters of multiple ribosomes attached to a single mRNA molecule. This structure allows several ribosomes to simultaneously translate the mRNA sequence into polypeptide chains, thus enhancing the efficiency of protein synthesis. Option A and B are incorrect because they do not involve mRNA, which is essential for the structure and function of polyribosomes. Option C is incorrect as peroxisomes are separate organelles with different functions related to metabolism and do not participate in forming polyribosomes.

questions.text: Plasma membrane is made of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is not completely correct. The plasma membrane, also known as the cell membrane, is indeed composed of proteins and carbohydrates in addition to lipids. The carbohydrates are often present as part of glycoproteins or glycolipids on the outer surface of the membrane and play roles in cell recognition and signaling.

This option is partially correct. The plasma membrane is primarily composed of proteins and lipids. The lipids in the membrane form a phospholipid bilayer, which provides the basic structure of the membrane. Proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer and have various functions, such as transport, cell signaling, and structural support. However, traces of carbohydrates are also present in addition to proteins and lipids.

This option is correct. The plasma membrane is a dynamic and selectively permeable barrier that surrounds the cell, and it is made up of three main types of biomolecules: proteins, lipids, and carbohydrates. The lipids, mainly phospholipids, form a bilayer, while the proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer or extend through it. The carbohydrates are often found on the outer surface of the membrane as part of glycoproteins or glycolipids.

options.text: Proteins, some nucleic acids and lipids
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While proteins and lipids are indeed key components of the plasma membrane, nucleic acids, such as DNA and RNA, are not part of the membrane's structure. Nucleic acids are primarily located in the cell nucleus or other specific cellular compartments, and they are not a fundamental part of the plasma membrane.

questions.explanation: The plasma membrane is a dynamic and selectively permeable barrier that surrounds the cell, and it is made up of three main types of biomolecules: proteins, lipids, and carbohydrates. The lipids, mainly phospholipids, form a bilayer, while the proteins are embedded in the lipid bilayer or extend through it. The carbohydrates are often found on the outer surface of the membrane as part of glycoproteins or glycolipids.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the fluid mosaic model of plasma membrane structure?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The plasma membrane's fluid mosaic model is characterized by a phospholipid bilayer, not a monolayer. A monolayer would only consist of a single layer of phospholipids, which does not apply to the plasma membrane structure.

This option is correct. The fluid mosaic model represents the plasma membrane as a phospholipid bilayer with proteins embedded within it. This bilayer arrangement, with hydrophobic tails inward and hydrophilic heads outward, allows for a dynamic and flexible membrane structure.

This option is incorrect. While triglycerides are lipids, they are not found in the plasma membrane as the primary structural element. The plasma membrane is primarily composed of a phospholipid bilayer, rather than triglycerides.

This option is incorrect. Triglycerides are not the main components of the plasma membrane, and a monolayer does not accurately describe the bilayer structure of the plasma membrane in the fluid mosaic model.

questions.explanation: The fluid mosaic model describes the plasma membrane as a phospholipid bilayer with proteins embedded throughout. This model emphasizes the flexibility of the membrane, as well as the diverse proteins that create a 'mosaic' appearance. The bilayer consists of two layers of phospholipids, with hydrophobic tails facing inward and hydrophilic heads facing outward. This arrangement provides the membrane with its fluid characteristic. Option A is incorrect as it suggests a monolayer, which is not the structure of the plasma membrane. Options C and D are incorrect because triglycerides are not the primary components of the plasma membrane; instead, phospholipids are the structural backbone.

questions.text: Which of the following acts as a receptor site on the plasma membrane?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The heads of phospholipid molecules are hydrophilic and face the aqueous environment on both sides of the plasma membrane. While they play a crucial role in forming the bilayer structure, they do not function as receptor sites. Therefore, this option is incorrect.
Cholesterol molecules help maintain the fluidity and stability of the plasma membrane but do not serve as receptor sites. Hence, this option is incorrect.
The tails of phospholipid molecules are hydrophobic and face inward, contributing to the structural integrity of the membrane. They do not act as receptors. Thus, this option is incorrect.

This option is correct. Glycoproteins, with their attached carbohydrate chains, extend outward from the plasma membrane. They are key in signaling and recognition as receptor sites, binding specific molecules like hormones and growth factors.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is glycoproteins. These are proteins with carbohydrate chains that extend outward from the plasma membrane, functioning as receptor sites for various molecules, including hormones and growth factors. In contrast, the heads and tails of phospholipid molecules are structural components, and cholesterol contributes to membrane fluidity but neither act as receptors.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the function of RER in eukaryotic cells?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While glycosylation involves the attachment of carbohydrates to proteins, this process primarily occurs in the Golgi apparatus, not the RER. Hence, this option is incorrect for describing the main function of the RER.
Lipid synthesis is a key function of the smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER), not the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER). Thus, this option does not correctly describe the function of the RER.

This is the correct choice as the rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is heavily involved in the synthesis of proteins. The presence of ribosomes on its surface is a distinctive feature that facilitates the translation of mRNA into polypeptide chains, which are precursors to proteins.
Ribosome synthesis is a process that occurs in the nucleolus of the cell nucleus, not in the RER. Therefore, this option is incorrect in the context of the RER's function.

questions.explanation: The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is primarily responsible for the synthesis of proteins in eukaryotic cells. Its surface is studded with ribosomes, which are the cellular machinery that reads mRNA and assembles amino acids into polypeptide chains, forming the basis of proteins. This is why Option C is correct. Option A is incorrect because glycosylation mainly occurs in the Golgi apparatus, not the RER. Option B is incorrect as lipid synthesis is a function of the smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER), not the RER. Option D is incorrect because ribosome synthesis takes place in the nucleolus, not in the RER.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is not true about the endoplasmic reticulum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: It helps in the transport of proteins between various regions of cytoplasm.
options.explanation: This option is not the correct answer. The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a complex network of membranes that facilitates the transport of proteins within the cytoplasm. Proteins synthesized on ribosomes attached to the ER are indeed transported to the Golgi apparatus for further processing. Additionally, the ER plays a crucial role in the transport of proteins between the cytoplasm and the nucleus, essential for various cellular functions such as protein synthesis and cell signaling.

options.text: It forms cytoplasmic framework for biochemical activities.
options.explanation: This option is also not the correct answer. The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) provides a cytoplasmic framework in eukaryotic cells, serving as the site for biochemical activities, including protein synthesis. Ribosomes attached to the ER translate mRNA into polypeptides, and the ER is involved in post-translational modifications that ensure proteins are functional and correctly folded.

options.text: It is involved in the formation of lysosomes.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect as well because the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) does play a role in the formation of lysosomes. The ER synthesizes the hydrolases—enzymes that break down macromolecules—packaging them into vesicles that eventually fuse with vesicles from the Golgi apparatus to form lysosomes.

options.text: It is involved in the formation of cell wall.
options.explanation: This option is correct. The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is not involved in the formation of the cell wall. The cell wall, primarily found in plant cells, is composed of cellulose and other components, and its synthesis is not a function of the ER.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is not involved in the formation of the cell wall. The cell wall is a rigid structure that protects and provides shape to plant cells, made primarily of cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectin. The ER is responsible for synthesizing proteins and lipids, as well as the formation of lysosomes by packaging hydrolases, which are crucial for digestion within the cell. The other options describe functions that accurately reflect the roles of the ER, making them incorrect responses to the question.

questions.text: Which of the following function is performed by both types of endoplasmic reticulum?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is primarily responsible for protein synthesis due to the presence of ribosomes on its surface. The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) does not participate in protein synthesis as it lacks ribosomes.

This option is correct. Both the RER and the SER provide mechanical support to the cell by forming a structural network. This network helps maintain the cell's shape and stabilize its cytoplasm. Additionally, they contribute to the overall structural integrity of the cell.

This option is incorrect. Muscle contraction involves calcium ions, which are stored in the SER. The RER does not play a role in muscle contraction, as it is not involved in calcium storage or release.

This option is incorrect. Detoxification is primarily a function of the SER, which contains enzymes like cytochrome P450 to metabolize drugs. The RER does not participate in the detoxification process.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that both types of endoplasmic reticulum (RER and SER) provide mechanical support to the cell. They form a network of membranes that help maintain the structural integrity of the cell. The RER is primarily involved in protein synthesis, while the SER is involved in lipid synthesis, calcium storage, and detoxification of drugs. However, neither protein synthesis nor detoxification is a function shared by both types of endoplasmic reticulum.

questions.text: Which of the following organelles is responsible for the production of steroid hormones?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Lysosomes are primarily involved in the breakdown of cellular waste materials and macromolecules, not in the synthesis of steroid hormones. They contain digestive enzymes that degrade proteins, lipids, and other macromolecules.

This option is incorrect. The Golgi bodies, or Golgi apparatus, are involved in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids for secretion or use within the cell. They are not directly responsible for the production of steroid hormones.

This option is correct. The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is the organelle responsible for the synthesis of steroid hormones. It contains enzymes required for the synthesis and modification of lipids, including cholesterol, which is the precursor for steroid hormone production.

This option is incorrect. The rough endoplasmic reticulum (RER) is primarily involved in the synthesis of proteins destined for secretion or for use in the cell membrane. It is not involved in the synthesis of steroid hormones.

questions.explanation: The smooth endoplasmic reticulum (SER) is crucial for the synthesis of steroid hormones, as it harbors enzymes necessary for the production of cholesterol and its conversion into various steroid hormones. This function is distinct from that of other organelles: lysosomes are involved in the degradation of cellular debris, the Golgi apparatus modifies and packages proteins and lipids, and the rough endoplasmic reticulum is dedicated to protein synthesis. This specialized role makes the SER essential in steroidogenic cells like those found in the adrenal glands and ovaries.

questions.text: Krebs cycle occurs in_ of mitochondria.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The matrix of the mitochondria is where the Krebs cycle enzymes are located, facilitating the cycle's biochemical reactions.
F1 particles are part of ATP synthase complexes found on the inner mitochondrial membrane, not involved in the Krebs cycle directly.
Stroma is the fluid-filled space in chloroplasts, where the Calvin cycle occurs, not the Krebs cycle.
Cristae are the folds of the inner mitochondrial membrane that increase the surface area for electron transport and ATP production, not directly involved in the Krebs cycle.

questions.explanation: The Krebs cycle, also known as the citric acid cycle, takes place in the mitochondrial matrix. This area houses all necessary enzymes for the cycle's reactions, which are crucial for cellular respiration and energy production. Unlike the matrix, F1 particles are associated with ATP synthase on the inner membrane, stroma is relevant to chloroplasts and photosynthesis, and cristae are structural features of the inner membrane, not sites of the Krebs cycle.

questions.text: Use an appropriate article:I bought _ new TV set yesterday.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: A is used before words that start with a consonant sound or singular countable nouns.
An is used before singular indefinite words that start with vowel sound.
It is not a specific tv set that is in question, so "the" will not be appropriate.
Article is required as it is countable common noun.

questions.explanation: "A" is used before singular countable words that start with a consonant sound. Also, it is not a specific tv set that is in question, so "the" will not be appropriate.

questions.text: Demonstrate the correct use of subject-verb agreement:_ anyone _ a pencil I could borrow?
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Pronouns, such as anyone ,refer to an unlimited set of things, therefore, using ‘DO’ before ‘ANYONE’ would be incorrect; as do is a singular verb.
‘DOES’ is a plural verb and thus can be used before ‘ANYONE’. ‘HAVE’ is a singular verb and thus can be used for ‘A PENCIL’. Therefore this is the correct option.
Pronouns, such as anyone ,refer to an unlimited set of things, therefore, using ‘DO’ before ‘ANYONE’ would be incorrect; as do is a singular verb.
‘HAS’ is a third person singular and thus is used with the pronouns he, she, and it etc. Thus to use HAS before A would be incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option A: Pronouns, such as anyone ,refer to an unlimited set of things, therefore, using ‘DO’ before ‘ANYONE’ would be incorrect; as do is a singular verb.Option B: ‘DOES’ is a plural verb and thus can be used before ‘ANYONE’. ‘HAVE’ is a singular verb and thus can be used for ‘A PENCIL’. Therefore this is the correct option.Option C: same reason as option A.Option D: ‘HAS’ is a third person singular and thus is used with the pronouns he, she, and it etc. Thus to use HAS before A would be incorrect.

questions.text: Comprehend key vocabulary:How we _ to ageing is a choice we must make wisely.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This is the correct answer because respond means react and so it makes sense in this statement.
Absolve means to declare someone free from guilt. This word doesn't fit in this statement hence this option is wrong.
Discharge means to officially inform a person that they must leave a place or situation. This doesn't make sense in the given statement and is therefore incorrect.
Overlook is a transitive verb and cannot be used before TO thus this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Option A: This is the correct answer because respond means react and so it makes sense in this statement.

questions.text: Demonstrate control of tenses and sentence structure:It seldom _ (rain) here in February.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: When talking about a particular month a present tense (RAINING) cannot be used hence this option is incorrect.
Rain is a phenomenon hence it cannot be used in this case.

Explanation:Tense:The word "seldom" means "rarely" and is used to talk about things that generally or habitually happen (or don't happen).For general truths and habitual actions, we use the present simple tense.The verb "rain" in present simple for "it" (third person singular) takes an -s: "rains".Sentence structure:"It" (subject) + "seldom" (frequency adverb) + "rains" (verb) + other information.Adverbs like "seldom" usually go before the main verb (except when the verb is "to be").
When talking about a particular month a past tense (RAINED) cannot be used hence this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Explanation:Tense:The word "seldom" means "rarely" and is used to talk about things that generally or habitually happen (or don't happen).For general truths and habitual actions, we use the present simple tense.The verb "rain" in present simple for "it" (third-person singular) takes an -s: "rains".Sentence structure:"It" (subject) + "seldom" (frequency adverb) + "rains" (verb) + other information.Adverbs like "seldom" usually go before the main verb (except when the verb is "to be").

questions.text: Demonstrate control of tenses and sentence structure:I_a strange noise.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: “Hear” is correct because in present simple tense we use first form of verb.
B and C are incorrect as they refer to a particular time phase, whereas there is no context given in the question.
B and C are incorrect as they refer to a particular time phase, whereas there is no context given in the question.
D “Hears” is incorrect as we use it with a second person pronoun. With a 1st person we will simply use “Hear”

questions.explanation: The subject in the given sentence is the “strange noise”. When referring to sounds perceived by ears, “hear” is used.

questions.text: Demonstrate correct use of article:There's _ little rice left.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The indefinite article "a" is used before singular countable nouns when referring to something non-specific or unknown."A little rice": This phrase indicates an unspecified quantity of rice, emphasizing that there is not much remaining.
"An" is used before vowels so this is incorrect.
"The" is used if identity of the noun is known to the reader and thus it cant be used in this statement.
This option is incorrect because the correct use of an article or a preposition is the requirement of the question.

questions.explanation: The indefinite article "a" is used before singular countable nouns when referring to something non-specific or unknown."A little rice": This phrase indicates an unspecified quantity of rice, emphasizing that there is not much remaining.

It would be incorrect to use are before collapsed.
Have is a plural verb hence it can be used for this subject thus this option is correct.
It would be incorrect to use were for something that has already happened.

questions.text: Choose the present indefinite tense form of the sentence:"He had been sleeping.''

options.explanation: He has been sleeping (Incorrect): Present perfect continuous tense is not applicable because the original sentence is in the past perfect continuous tense.

questions.text: Statement and argument:Statement: Should the public sector undertakings be allowed to adopt a hire-and-fire policy?Arguments:(I) Yes, as this will help the public sector undertakings to get rid of non-performing employees and will also help to reward the performing employees.(II) No, the management may not be able to implement the policy in an unbiased manner and the employees will suffer due to the high-handedness of the management.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Hire and fire policy' implies 'taking up the performing employees and discarding the non-performing ones'. Clearly, such a policy would stand out to encourage employees to work hard and devotedly to retain their jobs and thus enhance productivity and profitability of the organizations. Argument II seems to be vague in the light of this.

This option is incorrect because both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument II raises concerns about potential management biases in implementing the policy.

options.text: Both arguments I and II are strong
options.explanation: Both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument I is strong because it highlights a practical benefit of adopting a hire-and-fire policy: removing non-performing employees can increase efficiency, and rewarding performing employees can improve motivation. This argument is logical and directly supports the statement. Argument II is also strong because it presents a real and reasonable concern. In many public sector undertakings, management may misuse such policies due to bias or corruption. This may lead to injustice and insecurity among employees. This argument is also logical and relevant.

options.text: Neither argument I nor II is strong
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because both arguments have reasonable points, even though they also have some flaws. It is not accurate to dismiss both arguments entirely.

questions.explanation: Both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument I is strong because it highlights a practical benefit of adopting a hire-and-fire policy: removing non-performing employees can increase efficiency, and rewarding performing employees can improve motivation. This argument is logical and directly supports the statement. Argument II is also strong because it presents a real and reasonable concern. In many public sector undertakings, management may misuse such policies due to bias or corruption. This may lead to injustice and insecurity among employees. This argument is also logical and relevant.

questions.text: Statement and Argument Statement: Should admission to the professional degrees in Pakistan be given only on merit without any concession to any particular group of students? Arguments:(I) Yes. This will improve the quality of the professional institutes and degrees as they will be able to complete the degree successfully. (II) No. This will keep a large number of socially and economically backward students out of the reach of professional institutes and degrees.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Argument II is also very strong.

options.text: If only argument II is strong
options.explanation: Argument I is also strong.

options.text: If both I and II are strong.
options.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.

options.text: If neither I nor II is strong
options.explanation: Both arguments are very strong.

questions.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.
